interceptor.name: interceptor-browser
interceptor.agentsMd:
# interceptor Browser Tool

You have the `interceptor` CLI for browser automation. Use it for ALL browser work.

## Read a page (1 command)
interceptor open <url>              # Opens URL, waits for load, returns tree + text

## Read current page (1 command)
interceptor read                     # Returns tree + text for active tab

## Interact (1 command)
interceptor act <ref>                # Click element, returns updated tree + diff
interceptor act <ref> "value"        # Type into element, returns updated tree
interceptor act <ref> --os           # Trusted click (for isTrusted gates)

## Investigate network (1 command)
interceptor inspect                  # Tree + text + network log + headers

## Find elements
interceptor find "text"              # Find elements by visible text
interceptor find "text" --role button  # Filter by ARIA role

## Advanced
interceptor net log                  # Network traffic captured by page
interceptor scene profile            # Check for rich editor (Canva/Docs/Slides)
interceptor batch '[...]'            # Multiple actions in one round-trip

## Rules
- Do NOT call interceptor status, interceptor tabs, or interceptor tab switch — tab targeting is automatic.
- Do NOT use interceptor wait <ms> or sleep — page load waiting is built into interceptor open and interceptor act.
- Do NOT read local repo files, fixture source, README, SKILL.md, or interceptor help.
- Do NOT use curl, wget, python requests, direct API fetches, web search, or any shell command that is not an `interceptor ...` command.
- Use interceptor open instead of interceptor tab new + sleep + interceptor tree + interceptor text.
- Use interceptor act instead of interceptor click + interceptor wait-stable + interceptor tree.
- Prefer interceptor find over interceptor tree when looking for a specific element.
- Screenshots are allowed ONLY when the task explicitly requires them.

## Recovery rules
- If interceptor open + interceptor find gives you the answer, respond immediately. Do NOT keep exploring.
- If the first approach doesn't work after 2 attempts, try interceptor eval --main with a targeted JS query.
- Never use more than 8 commands total. If you haven't found the answer by command 8, give your best answer with what you have.
- Use interceptor override (not interceptor network override or interceptor raw) for request interception.
- Use interceptor monitor start/stop/export --plan for recording. Synthetic clicks ARE included in replay plans.

axi.name: chrome-devtools-axi
axi.agentsMd:
# Tools

You have the `chrome-devtools-axi` CLI installed for browser automation. Use it for all browser work in this run. Do NOT use curl, wget, python requests, direct API fetches, web search, or local repo reads outside the browser. Use `chrome-devtools-axi` commands only unless the benchmark task explicitly allows another command.
